title: ervatamia floribunda
intro: Ervatamia floribunda, also known as the "Chinese snake root" or "Evodia," is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving fever, reducing pain, and aiding in digestion, as well as having potential effects on cardiovascular and respiratory health. Ervatamia floribunda is typically prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or infusion, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine in the form of dried roots and bark.
